title: Connect Bluetooth Devices
summary: Pair wireless accessories like headphones or speakers with your device.
learnerPromise: Pair wireless accessories without guessing through settings.
whyItMatters: Bluetooth lets you use wireless headphones, speakers, keyboards, and more. Knowing how to pair devices means you can set them up yourself instead of relying on someone else.
realLifeUses: Connecting wireless headphones to listen to music or take calls.
Pairing a portable speaker to play audio aloud.
Using a wireless keyboard or mouse with a tablet or computer.
Connecting to a car audio system for hands-free calling.
youWillLearn: Turn Bluetooth on from your device settings.
Put an accessory into pairing mode.
Select the device from the list and complete the pairing.
Disconnect or unpair a device when you are done.
miniChallenge: Pair one Bluetooth device and test that it works.
steps: Open your device's Settings and find the Bluetooth section.
Turn Bluetooth on if it is not already.
Put your wireless accessory into pairing mode — usually by holding a button until a light blinks.
Look for the accessory name in the list on your device and tap it.
Wait for the word 'Connected' to appear, then test it by playing sound or pressing a key.
completionCriteria: Your device shows the accessory as Connected.
You tested the connection and it responds — sound plays or input works.
commonProblems: The device does not appear in the list — make sure it is in pairing mode and close by.
Pairing fails — turn Bluetooth off and on again on both devices.
The device was connected before but now is not — delete it from the list and pair it again.
tips: Keep the accessory within a few feet of your device during pairing.
If a button combination is needed, check the accessory's manual or label for instructions.
Rename paired devices in your settings if you have several and want to tell them apart.
